author: barnabasbusa
content: this is a PR to 5055

author: kevaundray
content:
also okay with this 🙏

I think what’s more important is what we set the defaults to be

author: jih2nn
content: Can nothing go wrong even when optional proofs validators become majority at the worst case?

author: barnabasbusa
content: they gonna start getting penalized if they miss many attestations

author: barnabasbusa
content: Nature will take care of the quick recovery if this happens 😄

author: barnabasbusa
content: I also do not see any node operator handling serious amount of ETH to willing to opt in for optional proofs tbh

author: barnabasbusa
content: The cost of them missing a single attestation with 5000ETH staked already is probably less than the cost of running that EL for a month

author: jih2nn
content: Won't they attest to an invalid block as well?

author: barnabasbusa
content: if they get no proof, they won't be able to attest no?

author: jih2nn
content: They don't check the result of execution engine nor proof engine, no?

author: barnabasbusa
content: zkboost would check the validity of the proof no?

author: jih2nn
content: Could you elaborate?

author: barnabasbusa
content: I thought the proof verification is done by the zkboost process

author: jih2nn
content: It's done right after the execution engine call. See L166 in your PR

author: barnabasbusa
content: what if we gate the validator's attestation on either the local el or verified gossiped proof?

author: jih2nn
content: What do you mean by gating?

author: barnabasbusa
content: validator won't be able to attest unless they have received the gossiped proof

author: jih2nn
content: In your scenario, you still don't want to rely on its validness, right?

author: taulepton_
content: Yes that’s current how it works in lh. We import the block optimistically if no EL exists (do not mark the block as valid or invalid). We then have a hook after successful validation of k/n execution proofs that sets the block to valid and as such ready for attestation.

author: barnabasbusa
content: updated the pr to add description about the gating validators

author: barnabasbusa
content: is everyone comfortable with this change?

author: taulepton_
content: I need to inspect in a bit more detail of the spec change but in spirit based on conversations I am

content: whats the timeline on trying to get the open cl spec pr merged in ?

author: barnabasbusa
content: as its all optional, we could even aim to have it rolled out on g*

author: barnabasbusa
content: we don't need to be so pessimistic as <@427491045308235776>

author: taulepton_
content: We hadn’t set a hard deadline but it would good to have specs finalised in the next week or so. I think all spec changes that may land are in open PR’s now.
